failure_surface: Formula applied to target range E2:E5 did not produce correct results because each cell contained an identical formula string with hardcoded row references (C2, D2) instead of relative references that adjust per row.
root_cause: When iterating through multiple rows to apply the same formula pattern, the agent assigned the exact same formula string to each cell. This caused all cells E2-E5 to reference row 2 data (C2, D2) instead of adjusting to their respective row numbers (E3→C3/D3, E4→C4/D4, etc.).
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When applying formulas to a multi-row range programmatically, generate unique formula strings per row that update the relative cell references (e.g., C2→C3→C4...) to match the target cell's row position.
evidence: Agent used constant formula string: 'formula = "=INDEX('"..."'"(C2)...(D2)"'"' assigned identically to each row in loop
Loop iterated range(2, 6) for rows 2-5 but did not modify C2/D2 references to C3/D3, C4/D4, etc.
Test case evaluation returned failure score of 0, indicating formula output did not match expected values
openpyxl preserves literal formula strings; it does not auto-adjust cell references like Excel's copy-paste behavior

memory_items.title: Relative References in Multi-Row Formula Application
memory_items.lesson: When applying formulas to multiple cells via programmatic iteration, each formula must contain row-specific references that match the target cell position.
memory_items.generalization: Build formula strings dynamically within loops using the current row variable to replace relative cell references (e.g., f'C{row_num}') rather than reusing a static formula string.

memory_items.title: Formula String vs Formula Object Behavior
memory_items.lesson: In openpyxl, assigning a formula string sets the cell value literally; unlike Excel, there is no automatic reference adjustment when copying formulas to adjacent cells.
memory_items.generalization: Always construct per-cell formula strings with updated references for multi-cell operations, or use appropriate library features designed for formula replication.

memory_items.title: Cross-Sheet Formula Validation
memory_items.lesson: Formulas referencing other worksheets require careful verification that sheet names and column mappings match the source data structure exactly.
memory_items.generalization: Before finalizing cross-worksheet formulas, verify the source sheet's column indices align with the formula's column references to avoid returning incorrect or #REF! errors.

edits.content:
## 6. Formula Iteration Patterns
- **Dynamic Reference Construction**: When applying formulas across multiple cells via iteration, construct each formula string with the current row/column variables embedded (e.g., f'`=INDEX(...C{row_num}...)`'). Do not reuse a single static formula string for all cells.
- **Library Limitation Awareness**: Libraries like openpyxl do not auto-adjust cell references when writing formula strings to multiple cells. Each target cell requires a uniquely constructed formula string if it depends on its row/column position.
- **Multi-Criteria Cross-Sheet Formulas**: For formulas spanning multiple conditions across worksheets, validate that column indices and sheet name syntax (including spaces/special characters) match the source data precisely before execution.
